expectations.evidence: Line 10 contains thesis field: "Meridian OS bets that intent-driven development — where AI assistants carry persistent project memory and execute structured, auditable workflows — will eliminate the context-switching tax that makes current AI-assisted development wasteful and inconsistent."
Timeline contains 3 horizons: near (line 24), mid (line 27), long (line 30).
All feature_refs in timeline use F-IDs: ["F1", "F2", "F3"] for near, ["F4"] for mid, ["F5"] for long. All feasibility entries also use F-IDs (F1-F5).

Feasibility section contains entries for all 5 features: F1 (low risk), F2 (medium risk), F3 (medium risk), F4 (low risk), F5 (low risk). Each has risk_level, technical_risks, blockers, sequencing_constraints, and architecture_impact.
All required top-level fields are present: slug, status, product_ref, thesis, narrative, timeline, feasibility, critical_blockers (empty array), open_questions, risk_summary, exclusions, assumptions.

Thesis uses product/strategy language (intent-driven development, persistent project memory, structured auditable workflows, context-switching tax). No implementation-level jargon like YAML, JSON, API, database, config files, or code references.
